latest.title: Application for Admission to Trading
latest.content:
Today 13:51
RNS Number : 4730D
Synthomer PLC
07 May 2026
Synthomer plc(the "Company")
Application for Admission of Further Securities to Trading
Further to the Company's announcement on 30 April 2026 of its final results for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the refinancing of its key debt facilities, the Company has allotted and issued 430,008 new ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the capital of the Company (representing approximately 0.26% of the Company's enlarged share capital following such issuance) (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") to certain of the Group's lenders in satisfaction of certain of the fees payable to them in connection with the refinancing.
An application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
LSE
") for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on the LSE's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
"). It is expected that Admission will become effective on or before 8.00 a.m. on 8 May 2026.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Annual Financial Report
latest.content:
Today 16:40
RNS Number : 6641C
Synthomer PLC
30 April 2026
Synthomer plc
2025 Annual Report and Accounts
Synthomer plc (the "Company") has today published and submitted the annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 for publication to the National Storage Mechanism in accordance with Listing Rule 6.4.1.
This document will shortly be available for inspection in unedited full text at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
The annual report and accounts are also available to view on the Company's website,
www.synthomer.com
, or on request from the Company's Company Secretary at 10 Greycoat Place, London SW1P 1SB.
The Company expects to publish the notice of annual general meeting 2026 and related documents in due course, in advance of the shareholder meeting which will take place on 22 June 2026.
This announcement is made in accordance with DTR 6.3.5(1A).
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI): 213800EHT3TI1KPQQJ56.

company_30s.what_they_do: Synthomer makes latex and polymer chemicals that are used in gloves, adhesives, and other industrial products.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global latex glove market is huge and essential for healthcare and safety. Synthomer holds a sizeable share of that market, giving it a key position in a sector that stays resilient even during downturns.
company_30s.current_state: After a series of refinancing moves and director changes, the company has tightened its balance sheet. It continues to generate revenue but needs fresh capital to sustain growth. The market has punished the stock heavily, leaving it trading at a steep discount relative to its assets.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively refinancing and adjusting its capital structure to shore up liquidity.
Management changes and governance tweaks signal a focus on stability and long‑term value creation.
Strategic communication around its core polymer business and supply‑chain resilience hints at a pivot to core strengths.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish optimism about a glove‑market rebound, tempered by caution over dilution.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight Synthomer’s strong market share, a tightening supply of latex, and a growing demand for gloves, especially in healthcare.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the need for a fresh capital raise, ongoing cash burn, and the threat of equity dilution that could erode shareholder value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from quiet to a surge of bullish posts, but the overall tone remains cautious as investors weigh dilution risks.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, suggesting no current hype; a sudden news event could trigger a surprise rally.

near_term_catalysts: A successful refinancing or capital raise that improves liquidity.
Announcement of new contracts or a price increase in the latex market.
A technical breakout above the key support area with high volume.
Supply disruptions that tighten the domestic glove market.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume surge on the 10‑day moving average, a break of support with a 20% volume spike, and a Q1 earnings report showing margin improvement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is already deep in a crash phase. The tight compression indicated by the APEX score suggests that a breakout could happen soon if liquidity improves.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash creates a large upside if the stock rebounds to the key support level. Synthomer’s core business is resilient, and a rebound in glove demand could push prices higher. The signal color (likely purple/green) indicates strong mean‑reversion potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Failure to secure refinancing or a persistent oversupply in the latex market could keep the stock flat or push it lower. Equity dilution and ongoing cash burn remain real risks.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is large—doubling or more from the current price if the stock reaches support. Downside risk is capped at the current level; the asymmetry favors the buyer.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce above the key support area with volume above the 10‑day average; set a stop just below that support; size 5‑10% of the portfolio.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a confirmed breakout of support on a 20% volume spike and a positive earnings note; then consider a small position.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable; no imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a steep crash, a looming refinancing, and a bullish glove market, this contrarian setup offers a high upside if the stock breaks support, but liquidity and dilution risks must be watched.
